_about: Generation config — the knobs the procedural visit generator (ShiftGenerator) reads to COMPOSE a shift's visits from the banks (book/postings/ciphers/drops/archive in references.json + townees.json + adventurers.json). Day 0 = the curated visitors.json (tutorial shift); day > 0 = generate_shift(day) with a seeded RNG (seed = day) so a week is 7 deterministic, non-repeating shifts. Output must match the visitors.json schema exactly. BANK D FILLED (Phase 2, 2026-07-13): weights, invalid-rate (+ optional per-day ramp), failure-axis weights, per-task composition contract, and sizable walk-in name pools all authored. See ../CONTENT-BANKS.md §Visit-Generation Algorithm.

shift.note: Generated visit ids are gen-d<day>-<n> (n = 1-based queue position), never colliding with the curated 16. `order` = n.

task_weights_note: Sum = 100 (read as relative weights; need not sum to 100). item_check leads because it is the mechanically richest loop — the ONLY task where BOTH the Glass (book_item) and the Scale (order limit) are load-bearing — and the one the curated tutorial teaches. rank_gate is the second pillar (fast rank/dues reads). The adventurer-directory tasks (rank_gate 20 + rank_up 8 + roster_change 10 = 38) draw without replacement from 16 adventurers → ~5.3 draws in a 14-visit shift, comfortably inside the pool. The townee tasks (dungeon_drop 12 + quest_file 12 = 24) draw townees → ~3.4 draws; quest_file needs an OWNER (11 of 16 qualify), dungeon_drop any directory townee. completion_claim + rank_up are the rarest (thin token/logbook material, and the pre-seeded fail actors are few) so they stay low to avoid obvious repetition across a week.
invalid_rate_note: Fraction of a shift's visits that are INVALID (a reject/hold/conditional under 4-verdict; a reject under STRICT_BINARY). ~55% clean approvals keeps the desk in flow (AGT.5) while giving reliable falsifiers. Scalar default; invalid_rate_by_day overrides it for a listed day.

invalid_rate_by_day._note: OPTIONAL per-day override — a gentle difficulty ramp across the week (mean ~0.45, matching the scalar). If a day is absent, fall back to `invalid_rate`. Generator: rate = invalid_rate_by_day.get(str(day), invalid_rate).

failure_axis_note: Weights are sampled ONLY among the axes a given task_type admits (see per_task[*].failure_axes) and renormalized over that subset — so absolute values matter only RELATIVE to the other axes a task allows. `identity` (herb/beast-part confusion) tops the table as the signature deduction; `authenticity` (forged seals/relics), `rank`, and `dues` sit just below as the everyday rejects. `dues` and `amount` are the two NEW axes this expansion adds to the existing enum. Rarer axes (unverifiable/claimant/paperwork/duplicate/reach) stay low because their triggering material is deliberately scarce in the banks (few pre-seeded dup logbooks, few no-card walk-ins).

item_check.actor_note: A courier/deliverer presenting an item against a standing order. Name from name_pools (a one-off walk-in); the ORDER is owned by a directory townee but item_check does NOT gate on that owner's dues — the order already exists and is paid.
item_check.subject: standing_order_item
item_check.subject_note: Pick a standing_order posting; its `item` is the claimed item; compare Glass->Book(item) and Scale->order limit.
item_check.failure_axes_note: authenticity only when book[item].forgery_glass exists; paperwork only when the order carries a `requires` (e.g. warded-transport-order's hazard seal). The generator drops an inadmissible axis and renormalizes.

quantity._note: How the generator derives inspections.scale.amount (unit = book[item].unit). The window is the order's limit in references.json — `accept {min,max}` or `total {needed}`.
quantity.valid_within: sample rng-uniform inside the accept window (or == needed for a `total` order)
quantity.amount_fail_note: over -> max+delta; under -> min-delta (or < needed). delta = rng.randi_range(1,3) in the order's unit.
quantity.decoy_when_not_amount: for a non-amount visit the Scale still reads a within-window quantity (decoy); relevant=false

rank_gate.subject: gate_posting
rank_gate.subject_note: Pick a posting carrying rank_min; compare actor rank vs gate and actor dues.
rank_gate.failure_axes_note: dues fail needs an `owing` adventurer (adv-nima-green / adv-perrin-saltcrag / adv-sable-thorn). unverifiable = a name_pools walk-in claiming a rank with NO directory entry (mirrors ganton-reeve) — the one rank_gate branch that uses a walk-in instead of a directory actor.

quest_file.actor_note: A townee filing/posting a quest they own (actor drawn from townees whose `owns` is non-empty). THIS is the primary dues gate: an `owing` owner cannot post.
quest_file.subject: owned_posting
quest_file.subject_note: Pick one of the actor's owned posting ids; prefer a ward_required posting so the fieldability branch has material.
quest_file.failure_axes_note: dues fail needs an `owing` owner (townee-sarai-quillon / townee-garrick-tallow / townee-old-perrin own postings). fieldability fail = dues current but no roster party carries the required ward/reach (mirrors sister-coll).

completion_claim.actor_note: Valid + authenticity branches: claimant = the posting's assigned party (resolve archive[token].posting -> its assigned_to). claimant-fail branch: claimant = a DIFFERENT actor (another directory adventurer or a name_pools walk-in), so payee != assigned party.
completion_claim.subject: completion_token
completion_claim.subject_note: A token in the archive for an assigned posting; valid iff seal genuine AND claimant == assigned party.

rank_up.subject: logbook
rank_up.subject_note: The actor's logbook archive summary vs references.json.rankup_thresholds[from->to]. distinct_seals >= threshold ⇒ approve. (Thresholds live in references.json, NOT here.)
rank_up.failure_axes_note: duplicate = distinct_seals < entries (pre-seeded: adv-castor-thorn 3/2, adv-sable-thorn 8/7). rank = entries < threshold, too few completions (pre-seeded: adv-lysa-ironquill 1, adv-tessa-hollow 2). dues = an `owing` submitter. The generator should bias toward these pre-seeded actors when it wants the matching fail.

roster_change.subject: transfer_seal
roster_change.subject_note: A transfer card from the actor's chapter; valid iff the card's mark+seal match cipher_table[chapter] AND dues current. Valid ⇒ roster_write.
roster_change.failure_axes_note: authenticity = a COMPOSED forged reading — borrow another chapter's `mark`, or swap the seal medium (wax->ink) — mirrors sable-lorn. dues (secondary) = cipher matches but the card's dues stamp is stale (an `owing` actor).

dungeon_drop.actor_note: A townee commissioning a fetch — ANY directory townee (the pure-commissioner townees cuthbert-lyle / goodman-fenn / elga-thorne / piers-danglow / mother-ashby exist for this; `owns` is not required). Dues-gated: an owing commissioner fails BEFORE the quote pipeline runs.
dungeon_drop.subject: drop
dungeon_drop.subject_note: A drop_table item; the quote pipeline is dues -> is_drop -> season -> reach -> payout arithmetic (payout formula in references.json).
dungeon_drop.failure_axes_note: dues short-circuits before the pipeline (needs an owing townee: goodman-fenn is the pure commissioner, sarai/garrick/old-perrin also owing). season = drop.season != the day's season (from season_schedule). reach = drop.floor > deepest roster reach_floor. identity = item is not a genuine drop (shop-craftable).

actor_pools_note: actor_pool vocabulary the generator resolves: `townee_walkin` = a one-off name composed from name_pools (no directory row; used by item_check couriers and the rank_gate `unverifiable` / completion_claim `claimant` branches). `townee_owner` = a townees.json entry with non-empty `owns`. `townee_directory` = any townees.json entry. `adventurer_directory` = any adventurers.json entry. `mixed` = per-branch (see completion_claim actor_note). Directory pools sample WITHOUT replacement within a single shift (CONTENT-BANKS §4.4b); walk-ins are unbounded.

name_pools._note: For one-off walk-ins (townee_walkin: item_check couriers, plus the rank_gate unverifiable and completion_claim claimant-fail branches) that are NOT directory actors. Directory-drawn actors use their authored names. A given + a surname are combined at random. 44 x 44 = 1936 full-name combinations — a week's ~30 walk-ins never obviously repeat a full name, and 44 of each keeps single-name recurrence subtle. All fictional, fantasy, no real people; deliberately distinct from the directory names so a walk-in reads as anonymous. No dead names or real surnames (Rule 7).

decoy_scales._note: A decoy Scale reading for a non-weighed subject (card / seal / token / logbook / filing). ShiftGenerator._decoy_scale(kind) looks up decoy_scales[kind] {reading, amount, unit}; the in-code match table is a fallback ONLY if the key is absent here.

rank_card.reading: A guild card in its case: 2 drams on the pan.

transfer_seal.reading: The transfer card: 3 drams of waxed board.

completion_token.reading: The token disc: 4 drams of good brass.

default.reading: Nothing on the pan; a petition, not a delivery.

season_schedule._note: Which season each generated day runs in (gates dungeon_drop). Day 0 uses references.json season.current (summer). day>0 walks the wheel so a week spans seasons and drop validity shifts. Every by_day value must be in `wheel`.

_validator_note: DeckLoader additions (impl phase): task_weights keys ⊆ the task_type enum; every per_task[*].failure_axes entry is in the (extended) axis enum; per_task actor_pool ∈ {townee_walkin, townee_owner, townee_directory, adventurer_directory, mixed}; season_schedule.by_day values ∈ wheel; invalid_rate and every invalid_rate_by_day value in [0,1]. See ../CONTENT-BANKS.md §Validator additions.
